message:
test(fuzz): add OpenFs MVCC WAL recovery fuzz harness + corpus

Lands a new libfuzzer target dedicated to the OpenFs::open WAL
recovery dispatch for ffs_mvcc, complementing the broader
fuzz_openfs_journal_replay target (landed in 6cdb2f1) with a
sharper focus on the `WalRecoveryReport` / `TailPolicy` surface.

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_mvcc_wal_recovery.rs (+515):
- `fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]|)` with MAX_INPUT_BYTES=256 and a
  128 KiB backing image. Builds an ext4 image with
  `EXT4_STATE_OFFSET`, `EXT4_JOURNAL_INUM_OFFSET`, and
  `EXT4_LAST_ORPHAN_OFFSET` manipulated to route the OpenFs::open
  path into WAL recovery, then writes a WAL via
  `ffs_mvcc::wal::{WalHeader, WalWrite, WalCommit,
  HEADER_SIZE}`.
- SeedCase enum covers Clean, TruncatedTail, CorruptHeader, plus
  the monotonic-sequence and fail-fast variants used by the
  `TailPolicy` surface. Each case chooses which TxnId / CommitSeq
  sequence to emit so the fuzzer can drive `TailPolicy::{Strict,
  Lenient, FailFast}` through valid + pathological boundaries.
- Tracks five logical block numbers (`TRACKED_BLOCKS = [1, 2, 3,
  10, 20]`) for per-block WAL write records and asserts the
  `WalRecoveryReport` / `WalReplayOutcome` output is stable when
  classifying the recovery.
- Calls `crc32c` on header fragments to ensure the fuzzer
  exercises `WalHeader` integrity paths without accidentally
  always producing valid CRCs -- the harness can flip the CRC
  byte via the byte-cursor to steer into `CorruptHeader`.
- Runs `classify_open` twice per input and asserts determinism,
  same as the sibling fuzz_openfs_journal_replay target.

fuzz/corpus/fuzz_openfs_mvcc_wal_recovery/ (+3 seeds):
- `seed_clean` (22 B): steers into the clean-WAL recovery path.
- `seed_monotonic` (26 B): drives the monotonic-CommitSeq path
  through WAL replay.
- `seed_truncated` (26 B): steers into the truncated-tail /
  TailPolicy path.

fuzz/Cargo.toml (+5):
- Registers `[[bin]] fuzz_openfs_mvcc_wal_recovery` between
  `fuzz_openfs_journal_replay` and `fuzz_ioctl_dispatch`, matching
  the existing path + doc=false pattern so `cargo fuzz run
  fuzz_openfs_mvcc_wal_recovery` picks it up.

No production WAL / OpenFs code change -- purely a determinism +
crash-hunting fuzz surface for the MVCC WAL recovery side of
journal replay.

files.patch:
@@ -139,6 +139,11 @@ name = "fuzz_openfs_journal_replay"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_openfs_mvcc_wal_recovery"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_mvcc_wal_recovery.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [[bin]]
 name = "fuzz_ioctl_dispatch"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch.rs"
